data.Modification: proline oxidation to pyroglutamic acid/Tryptophan oxidation to oxolactone/aldehyde and ketone modifications

project.title: Proteomic Analysis of USP17LA-Interacting Proteins in 293T Cells
project.projectDescription: To explore the interaction network of USP17LA, we overexpressed it in HEK293T cells and performed IP-MS to identify associated proteins and potential targets.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Protein bands from freshly CCB-stained gels were excised and placed into a 96-well microtiter plate. Gel slices were destained twice with 200 μl of 50 mM NH₄HCO₃ in 50% acetonitrile and dehydrated twice with 200 μl of 100% acetonitrile. The dried gel pieces were rehydrated in 10 mM dithiothreitol and incubated at 56 °C for 60 min, followed by dehydration and alkylation in 55 mM iodoacetamide in the dark for 45 min. After drying, the gel pieces were incubated in ice-cold digestion buffer (12.5 ng/μl trypsin in 20 mM NH₄HCO₃) for 20 min and then digested overnight at 37 °C. Peptides were extracted twice using 200 μl of 5% formic acid in 50% acetonitrile, and the combined extracts were dried under nitrogen. The resulting tryptic peptides were reconstituted in 0.1% formic acid and loaded onto a reversed-phase C18 column (Acclaim PepMap, 75 μm × 50 cm) on an EASY-nLC 1200 system. Peptides were separated with a gradient of 2–35% solvent B (0.1% formic acid in 98% acetonitrile) over 55 min, 35–55% over 6 min, ramped to 80% in 1 min, and held for 8 min at 300 nl/min. Mass spectrometry was performed on an Orbitrap Exploris 480 MS in data-dependent acquisition mode with an NSI source. The spray voltage was set to 2.3 kV, funnel RF level to 50, and capillary temperature to 320 °C. Full MS scans were acquired at 60,000 resolution (m/z 200), with an AGC target of 300% and injection time of 50 ms over a mass range of 350–1800 m/z. MS/MS scans were performed at 15,000 resolution with an AGC target of 100%, injection time of 50 ms, Top20 method, 1.6 m/z isolation width, 2E4 intensity threshold, and 30% normalized collision energy.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Tandem mass spectra were extracted using Proteome Discoverer software (Thermo Fisher Scientific, version 3.1) and searched against a mouse protein database with trypsin specified as the digestion enzyme. The precursor and fragment ion mass tolerances were set to 10 ppm and 0.02 Da, respectively. Carbamidomethylation of cysteine was set as a fixed modification, and methionine oxidation as a variable modification. A maximum of two missed cleavage sites was allowed. Peptide and protein identifications were filtered to a false discovery rate (FDR) of less than 1%.

references.referenceLine: Zhang H, Guo Z, Wei G, Yi J, Wang Z, Zhang Y, Zhao H, Ren T, Wang Y, Kuang J, Sheng Z, Zhang D. The deubiquitinase USP17LA negatively regulates T-cell activation and attenuates anti-tumor immunity. EMBO Rep. 2025 26(21):5300-5325

Characteristics[CellLine]: Transfected HEK293T cells were harvested and lysed in NP-40 lysis buffer (25 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, 1% NP-40/Triton X-100, 1 mM EDTA, 1 mM EGTA, 2.5 mM sodium pyrophosphate, 1 mM β-glycerophosphate, 1 mM Na3VO4, 1 mM PMSF) supplemented with protease inhibitors.
Comment[SpecilizedMS]: immunoprecipitation coupled with mass spectrometry analysis (IP/MS)
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: incubated with anti-Flag M2 magnetic beads
